title.zh: 名词：普通、专有、可数、不可数

summary.en: Nouns split into common and proper; common nouns split into countable and uncountable, with collective and material/abstract sub-types. Countability isn't a rule to memorize — it's about what you're actually counting. Nouns also take three possessive forms.
summary.zh: 名词分普通名词和专有名词；普通名词又分可数和不可数，并有集合、物质/抽象等子类。可数不可数不是死记的规则，而是看你到底在数什么。名词还有三种所有格。

heading.en: The two big classes: common vs. proper nouns
heading.zh: 两大分类：普通名词与专有名词

body.en: Everyday categories of thing that exist many times over — and that have an equivalent in every language — are common nouns ('rabbit'). Things that exist only once — Earth, Asia, China, Bank of China, New Year, or a person's name — are proper nouns. To mark a noun as proper, we capitalize its first letter.
body.zh: 日常生活中常见、世上有很多个、在各种语言里都有对应的类别名，叫普通名词（如 rabbit 兔子）。而世上只有一个的事物——地球、亚洲、中国、中国银行、新年，或人名——叫专有名词。为了表示专有，我们把它的首字母大写。

concepts.sourceRef: 普通名词和专有名词就是名词的两大分类

heading.en: English nouns carry number in their spelling
heading.zh: 英语名词本身能传递单复数信息

body.en: Unlike Chinese, where 'rabbit' alone doesn't tell you one or many, English changes the spelling: add 's' (with a matching sound change) for plurals — one rabbit, two rabbits; carrot, carrots. But the rule has exceptions: box → boxes, man → men, child → children.
body.zh: 中文里光看“兔子”不知道是一只还是多只；英语却靠改拼写来体现：加 s（读音也相应变化）表复数——one rabbit, two rabbits；carrot, carrots。但规则有特例：box → boxes，man → men，child → children。

concepts.sourceRef: 名词本身就可以传递单复数的信息

heading.en: Countable common nouns: individual vs. collective
heading.zh: 可数普通名词：个体名词与集合名词

body.en: Things you can count are countable nouns. The most common kind are individual nouns (rabbit, carrot). A second kind names a group of individuals — collective nouns like team, audience, fruit. Because a collective noun is a bundle of members, its number can go either way: 'This is a large team' (singular) but 'The team are working together' (the members).
body.zh: 数得清的名词是可数名词。最常见的是个体名词（rabbit、carrot）。另一类指多个个体的集合——集合名词，如 team、audience、fruit。集合名词是多个个体的统称，所以单复数要看语境：This is a large team（单数），但 The team are working together（指成员）。

concepts.sourceRef: 它们属于可数名词中的个体名词

heading.en: Collective nouns that lean one way
heading.zh: 偏向单一意义的集合名词

body.en: Some collective nouns are essentially plural in meaning — 'police' (the body of police), 'people' (a crowd). Others name one total whole and so feel singular — 'baggage', 'furniture' (all the furniture in one place is a single total, not two totals). Note: 'furniture' is uncountable because it's one whole, which is different from the uncountability of a substance like water.
body.zh: 有些集合名词基本只有复数意义——police（警察的集合）、people（一群人）。有些是总称，只有单数意义——baggage（行李）、furniture（某地所有家具是“一个总体”，不是两个总体）。注意：furniture 不可数是因为它是一个总体，这和 water 这种物质的不可数不一样。

concepts.sourceRef: 也有一些集合名词基本只有复数意义

heading.en: Uncountable common nouns: abstract and material
heading.zh: 不可数普通名词：抽象名词与物质名词

body.en: Some things can't be counted. Abstract concepts like peace and honesty can't — you can't say 'one peace, two honesties'. Material (substance) nouns can't either: water, air, beer, milk, wood, paper. We don't add 's' to count them — not 'two waters, three waters' for the substance itself.
body.zh: 有些东西数不清，即不可数名词。抽象概念如 peace（和平）、honesty（诚实）没法数——不能说“一个和平、两个诚实”。物质名词也不可数：water、air、beer、milk、wood、paper。不能加 s 来数——指物质本身时不说 two waters, three waters。

heading.en: Countability is about what you're counting
heading.zh: 可数不可数，关键看你在数什么

body.en: Don't memorize rules — understand what you're counting. 'Water' usually means the substance, but it also means a body of water, so 'two waters' / 'international waters' counts areas, not the substance. 'Two beers' at a restaurant counts glasses, not the liquid. To count a substance you count its container or unit: a glass of water, two glasses of water; a piece of bread, two pieces of bread — water and bread themselves stay singular.
body.zh: 别死记规则——要搞清在数什么。water 通常指物质，但也有“水域”的意思，所以 two waters / international waters 数的是水域，不是物质。点餐说 two beers 数的是杯子，不是液体。要数物质就数它的容器或单位：a glass of water, two glasses of water；a piece of bread, two pieces of bread——water 和 bread 本身不变复数。

concepts.sourceRef: 搞清我们在说可数和不可数时到底说的是什么

heading.en: Even proper nouns can be countable in context
heading.zh: 专有名词在某些语境也可数

body.en: A proper noun is usually uncountable because it's unique — but uniqueness can step aside. 'There's a Michael downstairs looking for you' treats Michael as one of the many people named Michael; 'There are three Michaels in my class' counts them. So we can't flatly rule that proper nouns are always uncountable.
body.zh: 专有名词通常因独一无二而不可数——但这种独特性有时会退居其次。There's a Michael downstairs looking for you 把 Michael 当作众多叫 Michael 的人之一；There are three Michaels in my class 则在数他们。所以不能一刀切地说专有名词一定不可数。

concepts.sourceRef: 在这样的语境下，专有名词也变得可数了

heading.en: The possessive case: 's, of, and the mix
heading.zh: 所有格：'s、of 结构，及两者混合

body.en: Nominative and accusative forms of a noun are just the noun itself (Rabbits love carrots). The possessive shows belonging or quality. Use 's most often after living people/animals — the rabbit's carrot, Michael's computer. For non-living modifiers you can use 'of': The quality of the paper, The brightness of the moon. The two even mix: 'I am a friend of Michael's' (one of Michael's friends). With 'and', shared ownership marks only the last noun (Tom and Jerry's room); separate ownership marks each (Tom's and Jerry's rooms).
body.zh: 名词的主格、宾格就是名词本身（Rabbits love carrots）。所有格表示所属或性质。's 最常用于有生命的人或动物之后——the rabbit's carrot、Michael's computer。修饰非生命名词可用 of：The quality of the paper、The brightness of the moon。两者还能混用：I am a friend of Michael's（Michael 众多朋友之一）。用 and 连接时，表公有只在最后一个名词加 's（Tom and Jerry's room）；表各自则各加（Tom's and Jerry's rooms）。

examples.text: A rabbit is eating a carrot.

note.en: 'Rabbit' and 'carrot' are common nouns — everyday categories with many members.
note.zh: rabbit 和 carrot 是普通名词——世上有很多个的日常类别。

examples.text: China, Asia, and the Bank of China are proper nouns.

note.en: Each names a one-of-a-kind thing, so the first letter is capitalized.
note.zh: 每个都指独一无二的事物，所以首字母大写。

examples.text: One rabbit, two rabbits; one child, two children.

note.en: 'Rabbits' takes a regular -s; 'children' is an irregular plural (not 'childs').
note.zh: rabbits 是规则的加 s；children 是不规则复数（不是 childs）。

note.en: Here 'team' is one unit — singular, taking 'is'.
note.zh: 这里 team 是一个整体——单数，用 is。

note.en: Same collective noun, but now it means the members — plural, taking 'are'.
note.zh: 同一个集合名词，但这里指成员——复数，用 are。

examples.text: Peace, honesty, water, and air are uncountable.

note.en: 'Peace' and 'honesty' are abstract; 'water' and 'air' are material nouns — neither is counted with -s.
note.zh: peace、honesty 是抽象名词；water、air 是物质名词——都不加 s 来数。

examples.text: We'd like two glasses of water.

note.en: We count 'glasses', not the water — 'water' itself stays singular.
note.zh: 数的是 glasses，不是水——water 本身保持单数。

note.en: 'Waters' here counts bodies of water (areas), a different concept from the substance.
note.zh: 这里 waters 数的是水域（区域），与“水这种物质”是不同概念。

examples.text: There are three Michaels in my class.

note.en: The proper noun is counted — three different people named Michael.
note.zh: 专有名词在此可数——三个叫 Michael 的人。

examples.text: The rabbit's carrot is on the table.

note.en: 's after a living animal marks ownership — the carrot belongs to the rabbit.
note.zh: 有生命动物后的 's 表所属——胡萝卜属于兔子。

examples.text: The brightness of the moon lights the path.

note.en: For a non-living modifier, 'of' is natural: the word after 'of' (the moon) modifies the word before it (brightness).
note.zh: 修饰非生命名词时用 of 更自然：of 后的词（the moon）修饰 of 前的词（brightness）。

examples.text: I am a friend of Michael's.

note.en: The two possessives mix: this means 'one of Michael's friends'.
note.zh: 两种所有格混用：意思是“Michael 众多朋友中的一个”。

prompt.en: Which word is a proper noun?

explain.en: 'Asia' names a one-of-a-kind place and is capitalized — a proper noun. 'Rabbit' and 'water' are common nouns.
explain.zh: Asia 指独一无二的地方且首字母大写——专有名词。rabbit 和 water 是普通名词。

prompt.en: Why is 'furniture' usually treated as uncountable?
prompt.zh: 为什么 furniture 通常被当作不可数？

text.en: It is an abstract concept like peace.
text.zh: 它是像 peace 一样的抽象概念。

text.en: It names one total whole, not separate counted items.
text.zh: 它指一个总体，而不是分开计数的个体。

explain.en: Furniture is the single total of furnishings in a place — one whole, not two — which differs from the uncountability of a substance like water.
explain.zh: furniture 是某地家具的总体——只有一个总体，而非两个；这和 water 这种物质的不可数不同。

prompt.en: Count the substance by its container: 'I'd like two ____ of water.' (fill the missing word)
prompt.zh: 用容器来数物质：I'd like two ____ of water.（填缺词）

explain.en: We count the containers, not the water — 'two glasses of water'. 'Water' itself stays singular.
explain.zh: 数的是容器而非水——two glasses of water。water 本身保持单数。

prompt.en: Make 'rabbit' show that the carrot belongs to it: 'The ____ carrot.' (one word)
prompt.zh: 用所有格表示胡萝卜属于兔子：The ____ carrot.（一个词）

explain.en: The possessive 's marks ownership: the rabbit's carrot.
explain.zh: 所有格 's 表所属：the rabbit's carrot。

quizzes.sentence: I drank two waters this morning.
quizzes.fix: I drank two glasses of water this morning.

explain.en: As a substance, 'water' isn't counted with -s. Count its container: 'two glasses of water'.
explain.zh: 作为物质，water 不加 s 计数。要数它的容器：two glasses of water。

quizzes.sentence: I am one of Michael friends.
quizzes.fix: I am one of Michael's friends.

explain.en: Belonging needs the possessive: 'Michael's friends', and 'one of' makes 'friends' plural.
explain.zh: 表所属要用所有格：Michael's friends；one of 使 friends 变复数。

note.en: 'Two beers' counts the glasses, not the substance — fine in context.
note.zh: two beers 数的是杯子，不是物质——在语境中没问题。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Of course. Is this for the whole team?

lines.speaker: Michael
lines.text: Yes, and bring a piece of bread for Anna.

note.en: Bread (uncountable) is counted by 'a piece of'; 'Anna' is a proper noun.
note.zh: bread 不可数，用 a piece of 来数；Anna 是专有名词。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Is that Anna's friend at the door?

note.en: The possessive 's shows the friend belongs to Anna.
note.zh: 所有格 's 表示这位朋友是 Anna 的。

lines.speaker: Michael
lines.text: No, there are two Michaels here, and she's looking for the other one.

note.en: Here the proper noun is countable — two people named Michael.
note.zh: 这里专有名词可数——两个叫 Michael 的人。
